terms.definition: The process of converting raw inputs into numeric feature representations, often via learned mappings such as text vectorizers or encoders, distinct from hand-crafted feature creation.
An estimator implementing fit and transform (and optionally fit_transform) to learn parameters on training data and apply a fixed mapping to produce feature matrices.
Transformer method that learns state from training data only—vocabulary, idf statistics, category levels—without emitting transformed output unless combined in fit_transform.
Transformer method that applies previously learned parameters to new data and returns a numeric feature matrix; must not relearn from the input batch at inference.
Convenience method equivalent to fit followed by transform on the same training set; appropriate for training rows, not for held-out or production data.
sklearn text Transformer that tokenizes documents and outputs a sparse matrix of non-negative term counts (optionally binary) over a vocabulary learned at fit time.
sklearn text Transformer that builds a vocabulary at fit and outputs term frequency–inverse document frequency weighted sparse features per document.
Stateless sklearn text Transformer that maps tokens into a fixed-size hash space without storing a vocabulary—useful for high-cardinality or streaming text.
Document representation ignoring word order; each dimension corresponds to a vocabulary token (or hash bucket) with a count or weight.
Sparse matrix with rows as documents and columns as terms (or hashed features); the standard output of text vectorizers.
sklearn method returning string names for output columns after fit, aligning human-readable token labels with matrix column indices.
